skill.name: maxhub-weibo
skill.description: Fetch and analyze Weibo (Chinese microblogging platform) public data including hot search rankings, trending topics, user profiles, posts, and comments through the MaxHub API proxy.
skill.summary: Weibo public data collection and analysis via MaxHub API
skill.author: MaxHub Team

security_audit.summary: This is a legitimate data fetching skill that provides documentation for accessing Weibo public data through the MaxHub API. Static analysis flagged 216 potential issues but evaluation confirms these are all false positives: backticks in markdown tables were misidentified as shell commands, environment variable access is intentional for API authentication, and network access is required for data retrieval. No malicious code or intent detected. The skill explicitly prohibits platform manipulation and only accesses public data.

high_findings.title: API Credential Access via Environment Variables
high_findings.description: The skill requires MAXHUB_API_KEY environment variable for API authentication. This is intentional and documented in the skill metadata.
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: Environment variable access is explicit and required for the skill to function. The skill metadata clearly documents this requirement and includes security policies preventing unauthorized credential use.

high_findings.title: External Network Access via HTTPS
high_findings.description: The skill makes HTTPS requests to the MaxHub API endpoint to fetch Weibo public data. Network access is required and documented.
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: Network access is required for data fetching functionality. The skill explicitly limits network communication to the configured MaxHub_BASE_URL only.

medium_findings.title: Documentation Contains Shell Command Syntax
medium_findings.description: The skill documentation includes curl command examples with environment variable syntax (${VAR}) showing how to make API requests. These are documentation examples, not executable code.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: Backtick syntax in markdown is being misinterpreted by static analysis as shell commands. However, these are documentation examples showing legitimate API usage patterns, not actual executable code in the skill.

low_findings.title: Static Analysis Pattern False Positives
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged 216 potential security issues (157 external_commands, 13 env_access, 5 network, plus blockers/obfuscation). Manual evaluation confirms all are false positives: markdown backticks misidentified as shell commands, legitimate environment access, and documentation formatting misinterpreted as code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Detailed manual review of all three files (SKILL.md, api-catalog.md, chain-patterns.md) confirms no executable code exists. All 'external_commands' are markdown formatting, 'obfuscation' flags are false positives from high entropy in markdown tables, and 'blocker' flags are misidentifications of API documentation.

content.user_title: Fetch Weibo Hot Search and Public Data
content.value_statement: This skill provides a unified interface to access Weibo public data including trending topics, hot search rankings, user profiles, and post content through a secure API proxy.
actual_capabilities: Fetch Weibo hot search rankings and trending topics
Search for posts by keywords, users, topics, or video content
Retrieve user profile information and post history
Access post details, comments, and engagement metrics
Fetch entertainment, life, and social ranking data
Chain multiple API calls for comprehensive data analysis
limitations: Only accesses public data via MaxHub API proxy
Cannot perform write actions (post, like, follow, comment)
Subject to MaxHub API rate limits and available endpoints

use_cases.title: Social Media Analytics
use_cases.description: Researchers and marketers can fetch trending topics and engagement metrics for market research, trend analysis, and content strategy planning.

use_cases.title: Content Discovery
use_cases.description: Content creators can search for popular posts, trending hashtags, and influential users to discover content ideas and understand audience interests.

use_cases.title: Brand Monitoring
use_cases.description: Brand managers can track mentions, monitor hashtags, and analyze user engagement around specific topics or competitors on Weibo.

prompt_templates.title: Get Current Hot Search
prompt_templates.prompt: What are the top trending topics on Weibo right now?
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner - retrieve current hot search rankings

prompt_templates.title: Search Posts by Topic
prompt_templates.prompt: Search for recent posts about [topic] on Weibo and show me the top results with engagement metrics.

prompt_templates.title: Analyze User Profile
prompt_templates.prompt: Find the Weibo user [username] and summarize their recent activity, follower count, and top performing posts.
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced - user profile analysis with chain calls

prompt_templates.title: Cross-Platform Trend Report
prompt_templates.prompt: Compare the current trending topics on Weibo. List the top 10 topics with their heat scores and category classification.

output_examples.input: What is trending on Weibo today?
output_examples.output:
Top 5 Trending on Weibo:

1. #ChinaEconomicForum# (Hot Score: 98.5M)
2. Spring Travel Recommendations (Hot Score: 87.3M)
3. #AIInnovationSummit# (Hot Score: 76.1M)
4. Spring Fashion Trends (Hot Score: 65.8M)
5. #TechWeek2026# (Hot Score: 54.2M)

output_examples.input: Find posts about artificial intelligence on Weibo
output_examples.output:
Found 12 recent posts about 'artificial intelligence':

Top Result: @TechDaily - 'New AI breakthrough in natural language processing...'
- Likes: 45.2K | Reposts: 8.3K | Comments: 2.1K
- Posted: 2 hours ago

[Additional results with metrics]

best_practices: Always check account balance before making bulk API calls to avoid unexpected charges
Use pagination limits (max 5 pages per request) to manage data volume and costs
Chain API calls strategically: search for IDs first, then fetch details in batch
anti_patterns: Do not attempt to use this skill for automated posting, liking, or following - these actions are not supported
Avoid requesting excessive amounts of data in a single query - use pagination and selective fields
Do not ignore rate limit warnings - excessive calls may trigger temporary blocks

faq.question: What data can I access with this skill?
faq.answer: You can access public Weibo data including hot search rankings, trending topics, user profiles, posts, comments, and various category rankings (entertainment, life, social).

faq.question: Do I need an API key?
faq.answer: Yes, you need a MaxHub API key configured as the MAXHUB_API_KEY environment variable. Sign up at aconfig.cn to get started.

faq.question: Can this skill post or interact with Weibo content?
faq.answer: No, this skill only reads public data. Write actions like posting, liking, or following are not supported.

faq.question: Is there a rate limit?
faq.answer: Yes, MaxHub has rate limits (default 60 calls/minute). The skill also enforces internal limits: max 5 pages per query and max 50 results per request.

faq.question: Can I search for specific users?
faq.answer: Yes, you can search users by username, get user profiles, view their posts and followers using the available user APIs.

faq.question: What is the difference between web and app API endpoints?
faq.answer: The skill provides both web and app versions of Weibo APIs. Web APIs (web_v2) generally return data formatted for web pages, while app APIs return mobile-format data. Both access the same underlying public data.
